name: Philadelphia Scrapple
recipeIngredient: 1 large Onion, stuck with 3 whole Cloves
All-purpose flour, for Dredging sliced scrapple
Butter, back fat or Vegetable oil for frying
recipeInstructions:
1. Place pigs knuckles in a large pot; add pork, onion, and water. Cook slowly, covered, for 2 1/2 hours.
2. Drain the meat, reserving the broth.
3. Chill the meat and remove the fat. Separate the meat from the bones.
4. Chop the meat.
5. Place the chopped meat in a kettle with 2 quarts of the reserved broth. Add salt, pepper, and sage; bring to a boil.
6. Combine cormeal with the remaining 1 quart of reserved broth and stir into the boiling mixture.
7. Cook over medium heat until thickened, stirring constantly.
8. Cover and cook over very low heat for another 20 minutes, stirring again after 20 minutes.
9. Pour the mixture into 2 (9-by-5-by-3-inch) loaf pans. Cool and chill overnight.
10. Cut into slices, coat with flour, and brown in butter or bacon fat.
11. Serve hot with fruit for a hearty breakfast.
